2 — Market Intelligence
2.1 Market Situation
NE (NJ)
Overall Market TrendCustomers are becoming increasingly price-sensitive due to overall inflation. In the Northeast region, we have seen growing demand for Natural Juices, Bubble Tea, and Tok Tok products. Meanwhile, sales of trendy items have slowed down significantly.
Customer Demand ShiftSome of our customers have adjusted their business model, as well as their order frequency and volume. Additionally, most customers are currently resistant to trying new items and prefer to focus on products that are stable and well-established in the market.
Regional DifferencesThe markets in NY, MD, PA, NH, and CT are showing strong growth. We have successfully introduced new items with key accounts across these regions. OKF, Ginger Tea, and Jellies continue to be strong performers in NY, NJ, and MD. In NH, Pocas items such as Bubble Tea, Popping Boba, Juice in Cans, and Horchata are driving solid sales. Juice in Glass remains our top-performing category in PA. In Canada, OKF and other trendy items are gaining good traction. Additionally, Popping Boba RTD is performing exceptionally well in MD, supported by our exclusive partnership. However, sales in VA have slowed overall and may require further evaluation and strategic adjustments.
Pricing PressureWe are facing competition across all regions; however, the most concerning situation at the moment is in Maryland. A competitor is offering OKF products directly from Korea to customers at prices lower than ours, which is disrupting the market. As a result, some of our customers have delayed or decided not to place container orders with us under the current conditions.
SE (FL)
Overall Market Trendlooking for more price centric products and private labels (V&V and Dismex and others) North and Central FL, lack of workers as per the immigration issue and make slow sales in that area
Customer Demand ShiftN/A
Regional DifferencesCentral FL is slower than other region since that area has more farm business and need immigrant help
Pricing PressureConchita, DMP & Lotty are strong and keep offering lower price
SE (GA)
Overall Market TrendMost of wholesalers are struggling with slow economy and gas price situtaiotn. C store and gas station sales dropped Lower the order qty and trying to manage inventory as minimun as possbile.
Customer Demand Shiftlooking for easy of comsumption and ready to consume products more popular
Regional DifferencesOverall struggling but GA maintain what they normally buy for OKF, Splash TN market slower than other region. They reducing order qty to manage inventory
Pricing PressureDMP is aggressive in the market Noodle brands' competition severe in the market. (Korean brands vs Japanese brands)
SW (TX)
Overall Market TrendThe Southwest grocery market continues to grow driven by population expansion, but remains highly competitive and price sensitive. Consumers are prioritizing value, promotions, and private label options, while retailers focus on price competitiveness, efficient supply chains, and strong in store execution.
Customer Demand ShiftConsumers are shifting toward value driven purchases, prioritizing lower prices, promotions, and private label products. At the same time, there is growing demand for convenient, ready to consume options and flavorful, innovative products that still deliver perceived value.
Regional DifferencesDemand varies by state, with Texas and Colorado showing stronger growth and more openness to premium and innovative products, while markets like NM, AR, OK, LA, and MS remain more price sensitive and value driven. Multicultural influence is stronger in TX and NM, driving demand for new flavors and product types (POCAS NEW ITEMS), while rural markets across AR, OK, MS, and LA rely more on traditional grocery formats and promotions (OKF).
Pricing PressurePricing pressure remains high across the region, especially in more price-sensitive states like NM, AR, OK, LA, and MS. Many customers are switching to lower-cost options, including private label brands from H-E-B and Walmart (Great Value), as well as more affordable brands like Viloe and Coco Royal. At the same time, higher gas prices, transportation costs, and overall inflation are making it harder for customers to spend. Because of this, shoppers are focusing more on essentials, looking for deals, and choosing cheaper products.
MW (IL)
Overall Market TrendEmerging brands such as Visvita, Mazuma, and Zanamia are entering the market with highly competitive pricing. However, many distributors tend to prioritize price over actual consumer demand and sell-through performance. Jellies remain in high demand, but limited inventory makes it challenging for some distributors to maintain consistent shelf presence.
Customer Demand ShiftCompetitive pricing, order support, and SPIF programs are key factors our partners consistently highlight in conversations.
Regional DifferencesIndiana has seen a demand in Splash, IL/KS/WI keeps increasing in OKF demand, MN/OH power our HGT sales with increasing demands
Pricing PressureCompanies have started their own private labels in some states, Competition always finds ways to undercut pricing even if it means digging into their margins.
WC (CA)
Overall Market TrendSlow growth with tighter margins (Rebecca mentioned during the meeting) and more competition. Even our customers started making their own Private Label (Diamond, Two Bros, Moosoo and Karabetian) and pushing us to develop more customers because this is something that we won't be able to control if customers pursue Private Label.
Customer Demand ShiftCustomers are prioritizing value, possibly moving toward more affordable or differentiated products (Like new Lattes and Dubai Mochis which means we need to keep developing something unique that no one has to be able to penetrate the market with the new trendy items).
Regional DifferencesDemand and growth vary by area but mostly stable.
Pricing PressureHigh! Driven by competition, cost increases, and customers pushing for lower prices or better deals.
NAT
Overall Market TrendWe are starting to see gettting more activity with our accounts: Burlington, Five Below, DD's, Homegoods, and Marmaxx. Growth is supported by new item development and ongoing review dates like the August rest . Focus remains on expanding product lines and maintaining relationships with buyers.
Customer Demand ShiftCustomers are shifting toward trendy, innovative, and ready-to-drink items, including Popping Boba RTD, Aloe Drinks, Mochi, and really Sour Candy concepts. There is also demand for visually appealing and unique packaging formats. At the same time, buyers remain sensitive to pricing and expect competitive offers.
Regional DifferencesDemand varies by account: Five Below focuses on innovation and flavor trends (Sour Challenge). Our Top 3 contributors for March 2026 is still California, New Jersey and Ilinois.
Pricing PressureHigh pricing pressure due to buyer expectations, vendor cost increases, and competition. Accounts are sensitive to price changes (e.g., Sour formula increase), and approvals depend on maintaining competitive margins. Private label and alternative suppliers continue to challenge pricing, requiring strong C&P alignment
2.2 Product Review
BranchMarket DemandCompetition (Brand / Price)Inventory IssuesKey Insight & Action
NE (NJ)Strong Demand specially in NY/NJ and MD. Customers have switched to Costa Rican Jellies after the issue with the Chinesse ones. We set up this item with important customers like Jetro, Kenover, and Whashigton C&C.We have a lot of Competion when it comes to Jellies . Our Biggest Copetitors are Prestige Concept selling $8.99 and Commercial Mexicana $8.99. Other brands like GL International, JB , Viloe, Buen Provecho are present in the market.We have been able to maintain a stable inventory level for Costa Rican jellies, while shipments from China have experienced delays for several months. This situation has been the primary reason many customers have been willing to accept the switch, despite the differences in quality. Howerver, the recent FDA detentions of incoming containers are likely to result in significant sales disruptions and potential holds.We have received numerous complaints regarding quality issues with the Costa Rican Jellies, including broken caps and water inside the jars. If we plan to continue working with these products, it is essential that the quality be improved in order to maintain sales and customer satisfaction.
SE (GA)Demand is good and looking for inventory Chinse animal with cup is the best selling but once we have Animal with fruit shape, we will see the market. There is a brand to bring Animal jar with fruit shape in the market alreadyplanet joy (made in china) provided by Triangle qualtiy has competitive price ($6.99) and aggressive in the market. as well, Buen provecho Pocas cost rica jelly has compeititve price point in the makret ($5.99)Animal jar with fruit shape already in the market and need to check the sales. We have one Cost Rica jelly container arrived but wait for the FDA And waiting for one cotnainer with fruit shape from ChinaOffering MDI, La Tortilleria, Diaz for the Animal jar with fruit shape and they need samples Once we have the cotnaienr, will push for the new Chinse Jelly Price isn't the issue as of now
SE (FL)Demands are good in the market. not a signal for the shortage from the customers or in the stores yetTrupi cups ($7.99) and Buen provecho ($9.99) in the market but don't see yet for the fruit shape in Jarin the market, not a sign for the shortage and our custmomers are not aware of this FDA yet.not for the price issue or competition. our Costa Rica jelly has good price point
SW (TX)Demand for jellies is strong right now. Customers clearly prefer cup shaped jellies, while fruit shaped ones are not selling as much. Presentation also matters a lot products that look better tend to sell faster. At the same time, there aren’t many options in the market, so customers are buying what’s available, even if it’s not always their first choice.There is a lot of competition in the jelly market. Main competitors like MAZUMA is selling around $6.99 jellies from China. At the same time, there is a national shortage of this product because the FDA. This is limiting supply in the market and creating challenges for all suppliers, while also putting pressure on availability and pricing.We are currently facing supply challenges. 2 of our containers has been sitting at the warehouse on FDA detention, which is delaying our ability to supply the market during a time of strong demand. This situation could lead to missed opportunities and potential loss of sales.High demand remains in the market, but supply is being limited by FDA detentions, putting sales at risk. Follow up closely to release detained containers, while securing alternative supply options to avoid stockouts and capture demand.
MW (IL)We are seeing strong demand from both existing customers and new prospects for jellies. Buyers are prepared to place orders with any supplier who can deliver product immediately.We are currently seeing a new competitor, Gushmello, a local Illinois-based vendor. Their jelly jars, sourced from China, are on shelves with a retail price of $14.99 per jar.We currently have containers on order pending FDA release. With shelves largely empty and customer demand increasing, this creates a strong opportunity for us to step in as a reliable jelly supplier.Price is currently not the main factor in the jelly jar market. While Costa Rica pricing was previously very competitive versus other suppliers, the current shortage has changed the dynamics. Right now, availability matters more than price, and suppliers who can deliver are gaining the business.
WC (CA)The demand for jellies on the West Coast is currently very strong. Customers are clearly leaning toward cup-shaped jellies, while fruit-shaped options are seeing noticeably less interest. There is also a growing preference for products from Chinese vendors, mainly driven by their appearance and overall presentation. Because of limited options in the market, many customers are currently purchasing Costa Rican jar products even though it may not be their first choice.In the market, Gemini is actively selling jellies sourced from China. It seems they were able to maintain inventory from before the FDA issue, which is allowing them to stay competitive. Their 3-cup small jelly jar is retailing at $4.99, which is setting a reference point for pricing in the current market.On our side, we currently have a container that has been sitting at the port for about two weeks. This delay is directly affecting our ability to supply the market at a time when demand is high, which could result in missed opportunities and loss of potential sales.We transferred FTL Jelly from NJ and it is already sold out.Overall, the market is moving strongly toward cup-shaped jellies with visually appealing packaging similar to Chinese products. There is a clear opportunity for us to capture more demand if we can align with these preferences.
NATDemand for jelly products is moderate across national accounts. Jelly is not the main focus for accounts like Burlington, Five Below, dd’s, HomeGoods, and Marmaxx, as they are currently prioritizing more trendy and innovative items such as Popping Boba, Aloe Drinks, Mochi, and Sour Candy.Competition remains strong, especially from lower-priced brands and Chinese suppliers that offer better presentation and competitive pricing. Private label options and alternative suppliers are also limiting opportunities within national accountsInventory challenges remain due delays on incoming containers, limiting our ability to offer consistent supply to national accounts.N/A

So far, only small orders have been secured, and it appears that additional time will be needed to further develop and position this item in the market.We have not seen competition for this item in our marketsNo Inventory IssuesWe need more time to present this item and position in the market.
SE (GA)Mochi Bag has better damand compare to the box one. Selling Mochi Bag and it' steady but not big volume thoughin Asian store has bunch of local brands but not at other storesgetting short dated as time goes (Aug and Sept 2026)need to focus on non Asian market. offering short dated inventory with the deal with all customers

SW (TX)We have been sent out small orders, and interest is still developing. In Texas, the product is moving very slowly at store level, so it will likely take more time to gain traction in the market.We have not seen competition at the stores we have this line,Expiration date (8/13/2026)Open more sales points
MW (IL)We have received some small orders, but sales are not moving as quickly as expected. This is still a product that requires additional marketing and education. While we have offerings to Hispanic, Asian, Polish, and Indian markets, only a limited number of customers are currently willing to take on the new productThere are currently no major competitors in the market.Short expiration date; inventory transferring from NJ. Product size remains a key challenge.Product requires more time in the market to gain traction.
WC (CA)The mochi bag category is gaining solid traction, with strong interest from both new and existing customers. Miniso has officially approved our Hello Mochi Bag (10 bags) and has already placed an initial preorder of 500 cases for the Dubai Style flavor. They are also planning to bring in additional flavors, although exact quantities for the other three SKUs have not yet been confirmed. Beyond Miniso, we continue to see consistent orders from other customers, and overall feedback on the product has been very positive, indicating good market acceptance.At Seafood City Supermarket, our Mochi Bag 16pc is currently retailing at $5.49. In comparison, Royal Mochi 12pc is priced at $4.39, while Mini Mochi 12pc is retailing higher at $5.99 within the same store. This shows a fairly competitive range in the category, with pricing influenced by brand positioning, pack size, and perceived value.We are currently waiting on a new container that includes the 12pc Mochi Bag format. The shipment is expected to arrive in April, and until then, inventory availability may be somewhat limited depending on demand flow.The momentum with Miniso is a strong signal that the Mochi Bag line has scalable potential, especially with the Dubai Style flavor leading the way. Securing and expanding this partnership should be a priority, while also preparing for additional volume once other flavors are confirmed. At the same time, maintaining competitive pricing and ensuring timely arrival of the new 12pc format will be important to support growth and avoid stock gaps as demand continues to build.
NATDemand for Mochi Bags is still developing across national accounts. Interest is mainly coming from Marmaxx , in which they are currenlty placing an orderr for mochis almost every week.Based on recent market research with Miss Jennifer, we identified key competition across NAT accounts. At Burlington (Secaucus), Royal Family Mochi (12pc) is retailing at $4.99, showing direct competition in the mochi category. At HomeGoods/Marshalls, Royal Family Mochi is even more competitive, retailing about $1.00 lower than Burlington.No major inventory issues at the moment, but there is mochi box currently with short dates in New Jersey. We are working in offer this with promotions.Continue building awareness with buyers and position Mochi Bags as a unique and trendy item. Also monitor competitive pricing (especially Royal Family) and ensure our value proposition is clear to buyers.

Customers are currently very cautious about trying new products given the economic situation.

As a result, no significant reorders have been received so far.Meiji Kinoko no Yama $4/99No significant reorders have been received so far.Continue pushing the item and aim to move all inventory before the hot season, as high warehouse temperatures could affect the product.
MW (IL)N/AN/AN/AN/A
WC (CA)We have successfully sold into our distributors and pushed the product aggressively, but they are facing challenges moving it into retail. One of the main concerns from customers is the chocolate component, as it is considered high risk for melting during transit, especially in warmer conditions. This hesitation is slowing down adoption at the retail level and limiting overall demand despite our efforts on the distribution side.At Super King Markets, Miltona is retailing at $3.69, although it does not appear to have wide distribution. The only widely distributed competitor in this segment is Meiji Chocorooms, with their 5oz bag retailing around $6.59. However, feedback from some distributors suggests that Chocorooms are not a top-performing item compared to other products like Hello Panda, which sells more consistently.At this stage, the main issue is not inventory availability but rather sell-through. Product is already in the hands of distributors, but movement at the retail level is slower than expected due to category concerns and environmental limitations.The biggest barrier for Mushhood is the chocolate-related risk, particularly around melting, which is impacting retailer confidence. Compared to competitors, even established products like Chocorooms are not strong movers, indicating that this category may have inherent limitations. Moving forward, we may need to rethink positioning, target the right channels (potentially less heat-sensitive environments), or consider seasonal strategies. Also we may need to reduce the pack size to smaller because some of our customers mentioned case pack is too big. It will also be important to support distributors with stronger sell-through strategies or incentives to help move existing inventory.
NATDemand for MushHood across national accounts is very limited. The item has been presented, but buyers are hesitant to move forward due to category concerns and low proven sell-through. Most NAT accounts are currently focused on faster-moving and less risky items, especially in the current economic environment.Based on market research with Miss Jennifer, there is very limited presence of this category in NAT accounts. The main recognizable competitor is Meiji but even these products are not strong movers in stores. Additionally, shelf space is being prioritized for more reliable categories such as snacks, beverages, and trending items, limiting opportunities for MushHood.No inventory shortages or issues.N/A

5.1 — Achievements vs Failures & Improvement Plan
CategoryKey AchievementKey Failure / GapImprovement Plan
SalesIncreased sales by 1.31%. Launched Ginger Tea Assorted Bags with Hana Food, Health Pioneer, and Constanza. Jetro buyer confirmed Assorted Packs for Bubble Tea, Fruit Juice in Can, and Latte Drinks. Sold a full truckload of short-dated items to Lewisco. Received positive feedback from King Kullen and scheduled an upcoming meeting. Continued moving short-dated inventory with support from Brother Produce. Successfully launched flavor extensions: Pocasville Chili Mango, Green Apple, and Splash Multivitamin 17.98 oz. May Flower introduced Latte Drinks. Presented Latte line to Cappabianco, and the buyer is currently reviewing the items. Received two containers of Popping Boba RTD from Hana Food.Have not been able to set up Mochi Bags with many customers. Ginger Tea Assorted Bag Overstock. (not voklume orders received) We have not open Convenience stores chains.Continue providing good Customer service (Good Offers, Inventory Managment, etc...) to maintain our current partners . And keep knocking on our prospects door to sucessfully open the accounts and offer the best items according to the customers Market and category.
New Items Set UpWe set up Toucan Coconut Water with Delphia and replaced the competition (Fruit Of Life). Also they accepted Yogo Ice Pop Offer and set up the item. We presented Bubble Tea and Poppinbg Bobba to Kenover for Shop Rite and received the first PO. Ginger Tea Assorted Bag set up with, Hana Food, Health Pionner, Constanza, We set up Pome Juice 8.45oz with Steward Leonard and keep getting reorders. Jetro Candy Buyer confirmed to set up Jelly Boy /Girl and we received new POs including that item. May Flower set up Latte Drinks.N/AWe will continue to offer the right products to the right customers, taking into account their market, demographics, and population.
New Account Open/RecoveredWe recovered Pacific Candy (Canada Customer) Receive a new PO. Open new account RM Variety.We are trying to get the 1st PO from : Nassau Candy Capital Foods And we are trying to recover : CAD (MD) Gromex (NJ)We will keep pursuing these accounts actively.

5.2 — Operational Issues & Suggestions
Issue AreaDescriptionBusiness ImpactSuggested Solution / Action
Clarification Needed on Purchasing vs. Imports ResponsibilitiesThere is currently a lack of clarity regarding the roles and responsibilities between Purchasing and Imports. As a result, tasks have been consistently routed to Imports without a clear understanding of whether they are the appropriate point of contact. This has created uncertainty around ownership, potential inefficiencies, and delays in communication. Clear guidelines and defined points of contact are needed to ensure proper coordination and smoother execution moving forward.The lack of clarity in responsibilities may lead to delays in decision-making, miscommunication, and inefficiencies in workflow. This can impact execution timelines, create bottlenecks, and potentially affect service levels and overall business performance.Define and communicate clear roles and responsibilities between Purchasing and Imports, including designated points of contact for each function.
5.3 — Support Needed from HQ
Support TypeDetails / RequestUrgencyExpected Outcome
Support for Inventory Control & Process AlignmentFrequent inventory shortages are being driven by high MOQs, elevated freight costs causing shipment delays, long lead times, and limited inventory visibility due to manual processes. Request support to strengthen inventory control, improve communication on ordering, and align on clear ownership to reduce stockouts and improve planning.HIGHImproved inventory accuracy and visibility, reduced stockouts, better planning of orders, and more consistent product availability across locations.

5.3 — Support Needed from HQ
Support TypeDetails / RequestUrgencyExpected Outcome
IMPORTINGWhile we understand the transition to new leadership and a new team within importing, the delays are creating significant challenges for customer container deliveries and our inventory planning.HIGHSummer is almost here, and we need the importing team to be fully prepared for the high volume of container orders approaching.
INVENTORYWhen it is time to transfer new items arriving in New Jersey, the NJ team has often already sold the entire container, leaving no inventory available for transfer.MEDIUMTo avoid inventory shortages, we will need to push new items only to selected customers until the product is fully established and inventory rotation improves, allowing for broader proposal and distribution.

CategoryKey AchievementKey Failure / GapImprovement Plan
New CustomersEl Campo (Sacramento), Blue Bead and Jons SupermarketWe received a business application from Kingman and hasn't gotten the 1st orderWe need to push and keep consistently follow up with prospects to close a deal and improve a relationship with them
New ItemsMiniso added Peelable Candy, Dubai Latte, Strawberry Latte and Dubai Mochi 10pc Jetro added Peelable Candy and adding Splash and Nectar San Diego C&C added Peelable CandiesWe may have inventory issue if our customers places larger orders and need to monitor and place orders accordingly We have been struggling with Mushhood and Sunrise Smoothies. Mushhood is not popular and pack size is too big and Sunrise Smoothie shelf life is too shortInventory Management need to improve and need support and trust from HQ. Slow Moving ItemsHave been closely working with Grocery Outlet and clearing out most of the short datedThey always ask us to deliver to Northern California and makes it harder for us to close a deal or reduce the priceWe need to rotate the products according to the expiration dates and sometimes warehouse team fails to follow. 5.2 — Operational Issues & Suggestions
Issue AreaDescriptionBusiness ImpactSuggested Solution / Action
Automated Daily Invoice ReportNo automated notification exists when orders are invoiced/shipped. Sales reps manually follow up with ops to confirm shipment status, creating unnecessary back-and-forth communication.Ops team interrupted by repetitive status inquiries; sales reps lack timely visibility into order fulfillment, risking delayed customer communicationSchedule an automated daily report from Acumatica listing all orders invoiced the previous day, emailed each morning to the sales team and relevant ops staff.
Hourly Sales Order SummarySales Order Summary report is run manually throughout the day. New orders entered by ops are not consistently reflected on our report sheets.Report sheets fall out of sync with actual orders entered; ops team loses time re-running the report multiple times daily.Schedule the Sales Order Summary report to auto-generate and email to ops/production every hour during business hours, ensuring report sheets stay current without manual intervention.
Physical Inventory CountA full physical inventory count is overdue but has been intentionally deferred. Conducting the count requires halting warehouse operations for approximately 2 days, and we do not want to risk impacting sales during an active push to hit sales targets.Inventory accuracy remains unverified, increasing risk of fulfillment errors, misships, and discrepancies between system records and actual stock. Continued deferral compounds the gap over time.Schedule the physical inventory count during a planned low-activity window to minimize sales disruption. Dedicate full warehouse staff for the 2-day count to ensure accuracy - no partial counts. Coordinate in advance with sales team to front-load any urgent orders before the shutdown period.

5.2 — Operational Issues & Suggestions
Issue AreaDescriptionBusiness ImpactSuggested Solution / Action
Communication with other DCsCommunication issues with other DCs; some DCs do not respond in a timely manner.Cause delay in quick response to buyers and carriers.Improve communication process, set clear response timelines, and escalate faster when delays occur.
Staffing / Warehouse SupportCurrently operating with one less warehouse staff member due to a knee injury.Slower warehouse processing, potential delays in order fulfillmentTemporary support, redistribute workload, or bring in backup staff to avoid delays when needed.
